summary: Matched three player bonus-library functions in `src/melee/pl/plbonuslib`: a full implementation of `pl_8003E4A4` plus small dataflow/type fixes that brought `pl_80040688` and `pl_800407C8` to 100%. The PR also corrected the `pl_8003E4A4` header prototype from `u8 slot` to `int slot`. decomp.dev reported 3 new matches and +1104 matched code bytes for GALE01.

key_files.role: Implemented `pl_8003E4A4`, moved `match_item_kind` before its new caller, and made matching fixes in `pl_80040688` and `pl_800407C8`.
Changed the `pl_8003E4A4` slot parameter prototype from `u8` to `int`.

what_changed: `pl_8003E4A4` was replaced from a placeholder with a C implementation using `Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2(slot)`, a `u32 seen[0x27]` local array, `match_item_kind`, and updates to `table->x0_staleMoveTable.x7AC[i]`.

`pl_8003E4A4` marks item kinds seen in the input `moves` array, increments per-kind consecutive counters in `x7AC`, calls `pl_80038788(slot, 0x9D, 1)` when a counter reaches the PR-diff threshold expression `*(u32*) pl_804D6470->x138`, and resets counters for unseen kinds.

`match_item_kind` was moved upward from below `pl_8003ED0C` to above `pl_8003E4A4`; its mapping stayed visible in the diff: kinds `0..0x22` map directly, `0xCD` to `0x23`, `0xE1` to `0x24`, `0xE2` to `0x25`, `0x28` to `0x26`, and default to `-1`.
`pl_80040688` changed the second argument to `unk_cond` from the caller parameter `arg2` to the stale-table-derived `temp_r0`, which is loaded from `temp_r3->x0_staleMoveTable.xCB8`.
`pl_800407C8` changed average inputs from implicit float conversion of `xCEC` and `xCF0` to explicit `(u32)` casts before assigning to floats and calling `pl_CalculateAverage`.
`plbonuslib.h` changed `/* 03E4A4 */ void pl_8003E4A4(u8 slot, bool, void*, int);` to `int slot`, matching the new definition and the PR body's `--require-protos` verification.
smart_moves: Reused the existing `match_item_kind` helper by relocating it before the newly implemented function rather than introducing a duplicate item-kind mapper.

Modeled `pl_8003E4A4` as a two-pass/three-loop counter update: zero `seen`, mark observed mapped item kinds, then increment or reset all 0x27 stale-move item counters. This mirrors the sort of simple loop structure that often matters for matching.
Matched two partially decompiled functions with very small semantic/dataflow adjustments instead of broad rewrites: `unk_cond(arg1, temp_r0)` in `pl_80040688` and unsigned casts in `pl_800407C8`.
Kept the header prototype in sync with the definition and verified with `python configure.py --require-protos && ninja`, avoiding silent ABI/prototype drift.
decomp_lessons: For matching, argument width in headers matters: changing `slot` from `u8` to `int` can affect call ABI, promotion/sign-extension behavior, and `--require-protos` cleanliness.
Inline helper placement can be part of the match. Moving `match_item_kind` before `pl_8003E4A4` allowed the new function to use the helper while preserving an inline-style local mapping.

When an int counter is consumed by a float average helper, explicit signedness casts can steer CodeWarrior/PPC integer-to-float conversion codegen; here `(u32)` casts were enough to finish `pl_800407C8`.
Trace temporary dataflow back to struct fields before assuming a call argument is reused. `pl_80040688` needed `xCB8`/`temp_r0` in `unk_cond`, not the incoming `arg2`.

The submitted matched source checks `match_item_kind(moves[i]) < 0x27` before indexing `seen[...]` and does not show an explicit `>= 0` guard. Future cleanup should verify assembly and valid inputs before changing that condition.
naming_conventions: Address-style public function names were preserved: `pl_8003E4A4`, `pl_80040688`, and `pl_800407C8`.
The local inline helper uses descriptive lower_snake naming, `match_item_kind`, even while surrounding exported functions remain address-named.
Header declarations keep the file's address-comment style such as `/* 03E4A4 */`.
Unknown or not-yet-semantic struct fields remain offset-style names under `x0_staleMoveTable`, including `x7AC`, `xCB8`, `xCBC`, `xCC0`, `xCEC`, and `xCF0`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a fixed-size local `seen[0x27]` array and loops over `i < 0x27` to align with the 39-entry item-kind/stale counter range in this function.

Preserve exact helper-call shape when matching: the PR diff calls `match_item_kind(moves[i])` in both the `< 0x27` condition and the subsequent `seen[...]` assignment rather than storing a visible temporary.
Use table-derived temporaries when they feed multiple operations: `temp_r0 = ...xCB8` is used for `unk_cond` and later `pl_80038788(temp_r0, ...)` paths in `pl_80040688`.
Unsigned casts before float assignment, as in `(u32) temp_r3->x0_staleMoveTable.xCEC`, are a practical matching tactic for average/ratio helpers.
The PR diff used explicit casts around the `pl_804D6470->x138` threshold comparison in `pl_8003E4A4`, suggesting a local type/codegen workaround in this slice.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals were present in the PR slice.
The PR body listed the intended matches: `pl_8003E4A4`, `pl_80040688`, and `pl_800407C8`.
The PR body verification command was `python configure.py --require-protos && ninja`.

decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 69.38% (+0.03%, +1104 bytes) with 3 new matches: `pl_8003E4A4` 0.00% to 100.00% (+616 bytes), `pl_80040688` 86.53% to 100.00% (+44 bytes), and `pl_800407C8` 81.90% to 100.00% (+31 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `pl_804D6470->x138` type cleanup or removal of the `*(u32*)` threshold cast around `pl_8003E4A4`.
Search for other `plbonuslib` average helpers where integer counters need explicit `(u32)` casts before `pl_CalculateAverage`.
Look for later semantic names for `x7AC`, `xCB8`, `xCEC`, and `xCF0` in `pl/types.h` or player bonus documentation.
Audit call sites of `pl_8003E4A4` to confirm valid `moves` entries relative to `match_item_kind` default `-1` behavior.
Compare other PRs touching `unk_cond` to see whether its second parameter is consistently a stale-table stored player/index value rather than an event argument.
